string.text:
Control how goodwill is weighted in comparative calculations. By default, goodwill with other factions than the trader faction is weighted less so actor would require a higher goodwill value with them to reach the threshold. This can be disabled by modifying the "Apply factor in" settings below.
 -Default: Only compares goodwill with given trader faction against the threshold number. This is the vanilla behavior.
 -Exclusive positive: Finds the highest goodwill with given trader faction -or- factions friendly to it and compares it against the threshold number. Works just as well with or without Dynamics relations enabled.
 -Exclusive negative: Finds the highest goodwill with an enemy to the trader faction and compares it against the threshold number.
 -Exclusive comparative:Finds the highest goodwill with given trader faction -or- factions friendly to it and substracts the highest goodwill with an enemy to the trader faction. The result is compared against the threshold number.
 -Total Positive: Sums up goodwill with given trader faction -and- factions friendly to it and compares the result against the threshold number.
 -Total Negative: Sums up goodwill with factions that are enemy to the trader faction and compares the result against the threshold number.
 -Total Comparative: Sums up goodwill with given trader faction -and- factions friendly to it and substracts goodwill with any faction that is an enemy. The result is compared against the threshold number.

Enable or disable the weighted goodwill factors for specific calculations.
 Enabled by default. Applies to the modes:
 -Total Comparative
 -Total Positive
 -Total Negative

Enable or disable the weighted goodwill factors for specific calculations.
 Enabled by default. Applies to the modes:
 -Exclusive comparative
 -Exclusive positive
 -Exclusive negative
Comparative goodwill affected by negative faction relations

Negative relation values between trader faction and neutral faction will (further) affect aggregated goodwill.\n E.G. if a trader's faction has a negative relation with a neutral faction around ~500, why would they care as much about the goodwill you have with them?\n However, at most goodwill is completely negated by this. It will build no negative number due to this setting.
Control how goodwill with the trader's faction is weighted in comparative calculations.\n
Control how goodwill with allies of the trader's faction is weighted in comparative calculations.
Control how goodwill with factions neutral to the trader's faction is weighted in comparative calculations.
Control how goodwill with enemy factions of the trader's faction is weighted in comparative calculations.
Dismiss goodwill value if negative on addition
Negative goodwill with factions neutral or allied to trader will not decrease total comparative goodwill value.
Dismiss goodwill value if negative on substraction
Negative goodwill with enemies of trader faction will not increase total comparative goodwill value.
